summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI (using DefaultAzureCredential, no API keys) to retrieve the 1985 New York Yankees roster and persists the result to an Azure Blob Storage container. The solution is Python-based, deployed on Azure Functions (Linux Consumption), provisioned via Bicep, and tested with pytest.
assumptions: Python 3.11 is the target runtime, consistent with existing Bicep infra memories.
Azure Blob Storage container name is 'yankees-roster', consistent with prior infra provisioning.
Authentication to TRAPI uses DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity); no API keys are stored anywhere.
The Azure Function uses a Timer Trigger with a nightly CRON schedule (e.g., '0 0 2 * * *').
Bicep IaC already exists under /infra based on repository memories; tasks build on and extend it.
CI/CD is implemented via GitHub Actions.
Application Insights is used for observability.
TRAPI_ENDPOINT and related config are stored as Function App settings (not secrets), since no API keys are used.
The blob output file is JSON, named with a timestamp or fixed name per nightly run.
Existing trapi_client.py and tests are complete; remaining work is the Azure Function host, blob writer, CI/CD, and infra wiring.
open_questions: Should nightly blob writes overwrite a single fixed blob or append a timestamped file per run?
What is the exact nightly trigger time and timezone for the Timer Trigger CRON expression?
Which Azure region and subscription should the resources be deployed to?
Is there a specific Application Insights workspace or Log Analytics workspace to integrate with?
What is the required retention period for blobs in the 'yankees-roster' container?
Are there any network restrictions (VNet, private endpoints) required for the Function App or Storage Account?
What is the TRAPI endpoint URL and GPT-4o deployment name for the production environment?
Should failed runs trigger an alert (email, Teams, PagerDuty)?
Is there a staging/pre-production environment required before production deployment?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Provision all Azure infrastructure, configure identity/RBAC, and establish CI/CD pipeline so the team can deploy and iterate safely.
entry_criteria: Azure subscription and resource group available.
GitHub repository access and Actions enabled.
TRAPI endpoint URL and deployment name confirmed.
exit_criteria: All Azure resources (Function App, Storage Account, App Insights) provisioned via Bicep.
Managed Identity assigned with correct RBAC roles on Storage and Cognitive Services.
GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs and deploys successfully to Azure.
TRAPI_ENDPOINT and TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME set as Function App application settings.

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the Azure Function Timer Trigger, blob writer module, and end-to-end integration so the nightly roster fetch-and-store flow works correctly.
entry_criteria: Managed Identity authentication to TRAPI and Storage verified.
exit_criteria: Timer Trigger function executes and fetches roster from TRAPI without error.
Roster JSON written to 'yankees-roster' blob container.
Unit and integration tests pass for trapi_client, blob_writer, and function_app modules.

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability controls (retry, error handling, idempotency), security hardening, and observability so the nightly job is production-grade.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met.
Function successfully runs end-to-end in deployed environment.
exit_criteria: Retry logic implemented for TRAPI and blob write operations.
Structured logging with correlation IDs emitted to Application Insights.
Alert rule fires on function failure.
No secrets or API keys in code or configuration.

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in production environment, confirm operational runbook, complete documentation, and sign off for go-live.
exit_criteria: Nightly function executes successfully in production for at least one full cycle.
Blob output verified to contain complete 1985 Yankees roster.
All success criteria from spec.md checked off.

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this task plan with the team and owner (rciapala). Confirm scope, wave sequencing, open questions, and assumptions before implementation begins.
outputs: Resolved open questions logged as GitHub issues or comments
acceptance_criteria: Task plan reviewed by rciapala and at least one engineer.
All critical open questions have a recorded decision or owner.
Wave sequencing agreed upon.
Task plan issue created in GitHub with label 'start-assign'.
risks: Unresolved open questions (TRAPI endpoint, blob naming strategy, nightly schedule time) block Foundation tasks.

tasks.title: Provision Azure infrastructure via Bicep
tasks.description: Author or extend Bicep modules under /infra to provision the Linux Consumption Function App, Storage Account with 'yankees-roster' container, Application Insights, and Log Analytics workspace. Wire identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage (no listKeys).
outputs: Deployed Function App, Storage Account, App Insights, Log Analytics workspace
acceptance_criteria: Bicep deployment completes without errors via 'az deployment group create'.
Function App is Linux Consumption plan with Python 3.11 runtime.
Storage Account has 'yankees-roster' blob container.
Application Insights resource connected to Function App via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting.
AzureWebJobsStorage uses identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName (no storage key).
Resources tagged with project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala.
risks: Subscription quota limits may delay Consumption plan provisioning.
Region availability for Cognitive Services / TRAPI endpoint must be confirmed.

tasks.title: Configure Managed Identity and RBAC for Function App
tasks.description: Enable system-assigned Managed Identity on the Function App and assign required RBAC roles: Storage Blob Data Owner on the storage account, and Cognitive Services OpenAI User on the Azure OpenAI / TRAPI resource. No API keys or connection strings used.
inputs: spec.md security requirement: No API Keys
outputs: RBAC role assignments deployed via Bicep rbac module
acceptance_criteria: System-assigned Managed Identity enabled on Function App.
Storage Blob Data Owner role assigned to Function App identity on storage account.
Cognitive Services OpenAI User (or equivalent) role assigned to Function App identity on TRAPI/OpenAI resource.
No storage account keys or API keys present in Function App application settings.
RBAC assignments managed in Bicep rbac module and re-deployable idempotently.
risks: Cognitive Services OpenAI User role may require elevated permissions to assign in the subscription.
TRAPI may require a different role or scope — confirm with TRAPI team.

tasks.title: Configure CI/CD GitHub Actions pipeline
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs pytest on PRs and deploys the Function App to Azure on merge to main. Use OIDC federated identity for Azure authentication (no stored secrets). Include Bicep deployment step.
outputs: OIDC federated credential configured in Azure AD
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on push to main and on pull_request.
pytest suite passes in CI before deployment.
Azure login uses OIDC (azure/login action with federated credential), no stored client secrets.
Function App deployment uses azure/functions-action or az functionapp deploy.
Bicep infra deployment runs before app deployment in the pipeline.
Pipeline status badge visible in README.
risks: OIDC federated credential setup requires Azure AD app registration — may need AAD admin approval.
Function App cold start after deployment may cause first-run failures in smoke test.

tasks.title: Implement Azure Function Timer Trigger (nightly)
tasks.description: Create the Azure Function app entrypoint using a Timer Trigger with a nightly CRON schedule. The function orchestrates calling trapi_client.fetch_1985_yankees_roster() and blob_writer.write_roster_blob(). Structured logging via logging module.
inputs: Azure Functions Python v2 programming model docs
Confirmed nightly CRON schedule from PROJ-01
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger CRON expression matches agreed nightly schedule.
Function calls fetch_1985_yankees_roster() and write_roster_blob() in sequence.
Structured log entries emitted at INFO level for start, roster count, and blob write completion.
Errors caught and re-raised after logging so Azure Functions marks the execution as failed.
local.settings.json.example documents all required environment variables.
Function runs successfully in local Azure Functions Core Tools environment.
risks: Python v2 programming model requires azure-functions >= 1.17; verify compatibility with existing requirements.txt.
Timer Trigger cold start on Consumption plan may cause occasional delays.

tasks.title: Implement blob writer module
tasks.description: Create blob_writer.py that uses azure-storage-blob with DefaultAzureCredential to write the roster JSON to the 'yankees-roster' container. Support configurable blob naming (timestamped or fixed, per decision from PROJ-01).
acceptance_criteria: write_roster_blob(players: list[dict]) function accepts roster and writes JSON to 'yankees-roster' container.
Blob name includes UTC timestamp or is configurable via env var.
Authentication uses DefaultAzureCredential (no connection strings or account keys).
AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME read from environment variable.
Unit tests mock BlobServiceClient and assert correct container name, blob name pattern, and content.
requirements.txt updated to include azure-storage-blob>=12.19.0.
risks: DefaultAzureCredential token acquisition may fail locally without az login — document workaround in README.

tasks.title: Add Function App application settings for runtime config
tasks.description: Extend Bicep functionapp module to include all required application settings: TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, TRAPI_API_VERSION, AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME, and APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING. No secrets or keys stored.
inputs: APP-01 and APP-02 env var requirements
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, TRAPI_API_VERSION set as Function App application settings in Bicep.
AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME set as application setting (not a secret).
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING wired from App Insights resource output.
No storage account keys or API keys present in any application setting.
Bicep deployment is idempotent — re-running does not change settings unintentionally.
risks: TRAPI endpoint URL may differ between environments — parameterize per environment in Bicep.

tasks.title: Write unit and integration tests for blob writer and function app
tasks.description: Extend the pytest suite with tests for blob_writer.py and function_app.py. Mock azure-storage-blob and verify Timer Trigger handler integration. Ensure overall coverage is at or above 80%.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_blob_writer.py covers happy path, missing env var, and blob SDK error cases.
tests/test_function_app.py verifies Timer Trigger handler calls trapi_client and blob_writer in order.
All existing tests continue to pass.
pytest --cov reports >= 80% coverage across trapi_client, blob_writer, and function_app modules.
Tests runnable locally with: python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=. --cov-report=term-missing.
risks: Azure Functions v2 Timer Trigger handler may require specific mock setup — consult azure-functions SDK test docs.

tasks.title: Implement retry and error handling for TRAPI and blob operations
tasks.description: Add retry logic with exponential backoff for TRAPI HTTP calls (e.g., tenacity or manual retry loop) and blob write operations. Ensure transient failures (429, 503, network timeout) are retried before the function fails the execution.
outputs: Updated trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py with retry decorators or logic
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI HTTP 429 and 503 responses trigger up to 3 retries with exponential backoff.
Blob write transient errors (ServiceRequestError, ResourceExistsError) retried up to 3 times.
Max total retry wait does not exceed 5 minutes (to stay within Function timeout).
Retry attempts logged at WARNING level with attempt number and wait time.
Non-retryable errors (400, 401, 403) fail immediately without retrying.
Unit tests verify retry behavior using mocked responses.
risks: Retry loops approaching Function timeout (10 min Consumption default) may cause silent truncation — set explicit timeout guard.

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights alerting and dashboard
tasks.description: Create an Azure Monitor alert rule that fires when the Timer Trigger function fails (exceptions or non-zero result codes). Configure a basic Application Insights dashboard showing nightly run status, duration, and roster count logged metric.
outputs: Alert rule deployed via Bicep or ARM in /infra
Application Insights dashboard exported as JSON
acceptance_criteria: Alert rule triggers within 5 minutes of a function execution failure.
Alert notification sent to configured channel (email or Teams webhook).
Dashboard shows: last run time, success/failure status, roster player count, and execution duration.
Alert rule and dashboard provisioned via IaC (Bicep or JSON) — not manually created.
Alert is suppressed during planned maintenance windows (if applicable).
risks: Notification channel (email/Teams) not yet decided — blocks alert action group configuration.

tasks.title: Security hardening review and checklist
tasks.description: Perform a security review of the deployed solution: verify no secrets in code/config, confirm least-privilege RBAC, check that the storage account and Function App are not publicly accessible beyond requirements, and validate Managed Identity token usage.
inputs: Azure security baseline for Function Apps: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/security-concepts
outputs: Security review checklist (GitHub issue comment or PR)
Any remediation tasks as follow-on issues
acceptance_criteria: No API keys, connection strings, or secrets found in source code, local.settings.json, or app settings.
Storage account public blob access disabled.
Function App requires authentication (system key or Managed Identity) — no anonymous HTTP trigger exposed.
RBAC roles are least-privilege (Blob Data Owner scoped to storage account, not subscription).
DefaultAzureCredential chain documented — Managed Identity is first in chain for production.
Security checklist signed off by owner (rciapala).
risks: Overly broad RBAC assignments from earlier iterations may need to be tightened.
Storage account firewall rules may break Function App if misconfigured.

tasks.title: Update README and operational runbook
tasks.description: Update README.md with project overview, local development setup (environment variables, az login, Core Tools), deployment instructions, and architecture diagram. Add an OPERATIONS.md runbook covering nightly schedule, alert response, and manual re-run procedure.
acceptance_criteria: README covers: project purpose, prerequisites (Python 3.11, Azure CLI, Core Tools), local run steps, environment variables table, and deployment steps.
OPERATIONS.md covers: nightly schedule, how to manually trigger the function, how to view logs in App Insights, and alert response steps.
All environment variables documented with descriptions and example values.
README pipeline status badge reflects current CI status.
Documentation reviewed and approved by rciapala.
risks: Runbook completeness depends on OPS-01 alert channel decision being resolved.

tasks.title: End-to-end production validation
tasks.description: Manually trigger the deployed Azure Function in production, verify the blob is written to 'yankees-roster' container with a complete 1985 Yankees roster, and confirm Application Insights shows a successful execution trace.
inputs: Azure Storage Explorer or az storage blob list
acceptance_criteria: Function triggered manually via Azure Portal or az functionapp function invoke.
Blob written to 'yankees-roster' container within 2 minutes of trigger.
Blob contains valid JSON array with at least 25 player entries (name + position).
Application Insights shows successful invocation with no exceptions.
Alert rule does NOT fire on successful run.
Nightly Timer Trigger executes automatically at scheduled time and succeeds.
risks: TRAPI rate limits or GPT-4o availability may cause intermittent failures during validation.
First nightly run may occur up to 24 hours after deployment.

tasks.title: Production go-live sign-off
tasks.description: Confirm all success criteria from spec.md are met, all tests pass in CI, monitoring is active, and obtain final sign-off from owner (rciapala) before considering the project complete.
outputs: Signed-off GitHub issue or PR comment from rciapala
acceptance_criteria: All functional requirements (FR1 Get Players, FR2 Store Players, FR3 Repeat Nightly) verified in production.
All spec.md success criteria checked off.
CI pipeline green on main branch.
Monitoring and alerting confirmed active by OPS-01.
Documentation complete per DOC-01.
rciapala has approved the go-live in writing (GitHub comment or issue close).
risks: Outstanding open questions from PROJ-01 not resolved may block sign-off.
TRAPI or GPT-4o service changes could affect roster completeness post-launch.
